title: fritillaria obliqua subsp. obliqua
intro: Fritillaria obliqua subsp. obliqua is a species of flowering plant in the lily family, characterized by its unique, bell-shaped flowers that typically bloom in shades of purple and white. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine and veratridine. The health benefits of Fritillaria obliqua subsp. obliqua include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often combined with other ingredients and dried to be used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
